# Rails 8.x — Tech Pack

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `authenticity_token` | HTML form hidden field | `<input type="hidden" name="authenticity_token" ...>` | High — Rails CSRF |
| `_session_id` | Cookie name | `_session_id=...` | High — default Rails session cookie |
| `X-Runtime` | Response header | Decimal seconds, e.g. `0.043721` | High — Rails default |
| `X-Request-Id` | Response header | UUID string | Medium — Rails default |
| `ETag` | Response header | Quoted hash string | Medium — Rails default |
| `"Ruby on Rails"` | Wappalyzer result | Technology fingerprint | High — Wappalyzer detection |
| `X-Powered-By` | Response header | May reveal Rails version | Low — often absent in production |

**Version extraction:**

```bash
# Sometimes reveals version via X-Powered-By (rare in production):
curl -s https://site/ -I | grep X-Powered-By

# Development only — explicit version info page:
curl -s https://site/rails/info/properties
```

Better signals: error pages in development mode, or `GET /rails/info/properties` if accessible (returns 403 in production on a correctly-configured app).

## 2. Default API Surfaces

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `/rails/info/properties` | GET | None (dev) / 403 (prod) | Rails version, env details — dev only |
| `/rails/info/routes` | GET | None (dev) / 403 (prod) | Full route table — dev only |
| `/rails/mailers` | GET | None (dev) / 403 (prod) | Mailer preview index — dev only |
| `/api/v1/` | GET | Varies | Rails API mode convention — not guaranteed |
| `/{resource}` | GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE | Varies | REST conventions: `/users`, `/posts`, etc. |
| `/{resource}.json` | GET | Varies | Format-suffixed JSON — same controller, JSON format |
| `/users/sign_in` | GET/POST | None / Session | Devise login — GET returns form, POST submits credentials (model prefix may differ) |
| `/users/sign_up` | GET/POST | None | Devise registration (model prefix may differ) |
| `/users/sign_out` | DELETE | Session | Devise logout |
| `/users/password/new` | GET/POST | None | Devise password reset flow |
| `/sidekiq` | GET | Often password-protected | Sidekiq Web UI, mounted in routes.rb |
| `/letter_opener` | GET | None (dev only) | LetterOpener dev email preview |
| `/cable` | WS | Session / token | Action Cable WebSocket endpoint |
| `/admin/` | GET | Varies | ActiveAdmin dashboard if gem mounted |

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | How to access | Contains |
|----------|---------------|----------|
| `config/credentials.yml.enc` | Server-side only; decrypted with `RAILS_MASTER_KEY` | Database passwords, API keys, secrets |
| `config/database.yml` | Server-side only | DB adapter, host, database name, pool size |
| `<meta name="csrf-token">` in `<head>` | `curl -s https://site/ \| grep csrf-token` | Per-session CSRF token |
| `authenticity_token` in forms | `curl -s https://site/users/sign_in \| grep authenticity_token` | Per-session CSRF token (form variant) |
| Asset manifest (Propshaft) | `/assets/manifest-{hash}.json` | Digest map of asset filenames |
| Asset manifest (Sprockets) | `/assets/` directory listing (if indexes enabled) | Fingerprinted JS/CSS paths |
| `<script type="importmap">` | Parsed from any HTML page | All JS module paths (Rails 7-8 default) |

## 4. Auth Patterns

| Pattern | Location | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| CSRF token (form) | `<input type="hidden" name="authenticity_token">` | Must be sent with all non-GET requests |
| CSRF token (header) | `<meta name="csrf-token" content="...">` in `<head>` | Send as `X-CSRF-Token` header |
| Session cookie | `_session_id` cookie (encrypted) | Opaque; acquire via cookie jar |
| Devise session | `/users/sign_in` POST → Set-Cookie | Most common Rails auth gem |
| JWT (knock / jwt_sessions) | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` | Common in API-only Rails apps |
| API key | `X-API-Key` or `Authorization: Token token=<key>` | Custom header, app-defined |

**CSRF token acquisition:**

```bash
# From meta tag (recommended — works on any page load):
CSRF=$(curl -sc /tmp/cookies.txt https://site/ \
  | grep -o 'content="[^"]*" name="csrf-token"' \
  | grep -o 'content="[^"]*"' \
  | cut -d'"' -f2)

# From a sign-in form hidden input:
CSRF=$(curl -s https://site/users/sign_in \
  | grep -o 'value="[^"]*" name="authenticity_token"' \
  | grep -o 'value="[^"]*"' \
  | cut -d'"' -f2)
```

The CSRF token is per-session, not per-request. Fetch it once per session and reuse.

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `/assets/application-{digest}.js` | Main JS bundle (Sprockets or Propshaft) |
| `/assets/{name}-{digest}.js` | Individual asset chunks |
| `app/javascript/controllers/` | Stimulus controllers (Hotwire) |
| `app/javascript/` | Application-level JS source |
| `<script type="importmap">` in HTML | All JS module paths — read to discover entry points |

**Rails 8 uses Propshaft (new) or Sprockets (legacy).** Hashed filenames mean you must extract the real filename from the HTML before fetching:

```bash
# Step 1: Extract the actual hashed filename from the HTML source:
ASSET_URL=$(curl -s https://site/ | grep -o '/assets/application-[a-f0-9]*.js' | head -1)

# Step 2: Fetch the resolved asset:
curl -s "https://site${ASSET_URL}"
```

**Import maps (Rails 7-8 default) expose all JS entry points directly in the HTML:**

```bash
# Extract and pretty-print the import map JSON from the HTML:
curl -s https://site/ | grep -o '"importmap">[^<]*</script>' | python3 -m json.tool
```

## 6. Source Map Patterns

Rails asset pipelines (Propshaft and Sprockets) do not generate source maps by default in production.

- **Propshaft:** No source maps generated by default. Asset files are digest-renamed only.
- **Sprockets:** No source maps in production. Development may expose unminified assets.
- **Vite-Rails (vite_ruby gem):** May generate source maps if `sourcemap: true` is set in `vite.config.ts`.
- **cssbundling-rails / jsbundling-rails:** Depends on bundler config (esbuild, rollup, webpack). Source maps follow bundler settings.

**Check for source maps after resolving the real asset filename:**

```bash
# Step 1: Resolve hashed filename from HTML:
ASSET_URL=$(curl -s https://site/ | grep -o '/assets/application-[a-f0-9]*.js' | head -1)

# Step 2: Probe for a .map file at the same path:
curl -I "https://site${ASSET_URL}.map"
```

Expect `404` in production for Propshaft/Sprockets apps. A `200` indicates a misconfigured or development build was deployed.

## 7. Common Plugins & Extensions

| Gem | API it adds | Detection signal |
|-----|-------------|-----------------|
| `devise` | `/users/sign_in`, `/users/sign_up`, `/users/sign_out`, `/users/password/new` | Routes present; `devise` in HTML forms or JS |
| `pundit` | None (server-side authorization only) | `403 Forbidden` with authorization message |
| `cancancan` | None (server-side authorization only) | `403 Forbidden`; `ability` references in errors |
| `activeadmin` | `/admin/` dashboard, `/admin/{resource}` | Redirect to `/admin/login`; ActiveAdmin HTML structure |
| `sidekiq` | `/sidekiq` Web UI | `Sidekiq` in page title; requires auth if configured |
| `action_cable` | `/cable` WebSocket | `<action-cable-consumer>` or `createConsumer` in JS |
| `hotwire-rails` (turbo + stimulus) | Turbo Drive, Turbo Frames, Turbo Streams | `data-turbo`, `<turbo-frame>` elements in HTML |
| `grape` | Custom API mounts, often `/api/v1/` | Grape-specific error format (`{"error":"..."}`) |
| `jsonapi-resources` | JSON:API at `/api/` | `application/vnd.api+json` content-type |
| `letter_opener` | `/letter_opener` email preview (dev) | Accessible in development only; 404 in production |

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `<script type="importmap">` in HTML | All JS module names and paths | Present on every page in Rails 7-8 apps using importmap-rails |
| `/{resource}.json` | JSON representation of resource | Only if `.json` format is enabled in the controller |
| `/robots.txt` | Disallowed paths, crawl hints | Often reveals admin paths and protected routes |
| `/sitemap.xml` | All public page URLs | Present only if a sitemap gem (e.g. `sitemap_generator`) is installed |
| `/rails/info/properties` | Rails version, Ruby version, environment | Dev only — returns 200; production returns 403 |
| `/rails/info/routes` | Full named routes and HTTP methods | Dev only — invaluable for mapping the app surface |

## 9. Probe Checklist

- [ ] `curl -I https://site/` — check for `X-Runtime`, `X-Request-Id`, `X-Powered-By` headers
- [ ] `curl -s https://site/ | grep csrf-token` — extract CSRF token from meta tag
- [ ] `GET /rails/info/properties` — 200 means dev/misconfigured (read everything); 403 = production
- [ ] `GET /rails/info/routes` — 200 means dev/misconfigured; full route table exposed
- [ ] `GET /users/sign_in` — Devise present? Note the model name (users, accounts, admins, etc.)
- [ ] `GET /admin/` — ActiveAdmin mounted? Note redirect target for login
- [ ] `GET /sidekiq` — Sidekiq Web UI? 401/403 = exists but protected; 404 = not mounted
- [ ] `curl -s https://site/ | grep -o '<script type="importmap">.*</script>'` — parse import map for all JS module paths
- [ ] `GET /{resource}.json` — probe common REST resources (`/users.json`, `/posts.json`, `/articles.json`)
- [ ] `GET /cable` — Action Cable endpoint present? HTTP returns 426 Upgrade Required (expected)
- [ ] `GET /api/v1/` — API mode routes; try with `Accept: application/json` header
- [ ] `curl -s https://site/ | grep -o 'data-turbo\|<turbo-frame'` — Hotwire/Turbo present?`
- [ ] Check for Hotwire Turbo Stream endpoints — look for `Turbo-Stream` headers or `/turbo/` paths in network traffic
- [ ] Extract CSP nonce from import map script tag — `document.querySelector('script[type="importmap"]')` for nonce patterns
- [ ] Detect Action Cable WebSocket endpoints — check for `/cable` or `ActionCable` in JS bundles
- [ ] `GET /assets/application-*.js` — extract actual filename from HTML source, check for source maps
- [ ] Check for `config/master.key` or `credentials.yml.enc` exposure (common misconfiguration)
- [ ] `GET /up` or `GET /health` — check for health check endpoints (common in Rails API mode)

## 10. Gotchas

- `/rails/info/properties` and `/rails/info/routes` are only accessible in development. A 403 in production is expected and correct. If you get a 200 in production, the app is misconfigured — read every detail before the operator notices.
- The CSRF token is per-session, not per-request. Fetch it once by loading any page with `Set-Cookie` response, store the session cookie, and reuse the token for all non-GET requests in that session.
- Rails format negotiation: the same URL returns HTML or JSON depending on the `Accept` header or a `.json` suffix. Always probe endpoints with `Accept: application/json` to check if a JSON API exists at routes you'd expect to return HTML only.
- Import maps (default in Rails 7-8) expose every JS entry point directly in the HTML `<script type="importmap">` block. Parsing it reveals all application modules and their cache-busted paths — far more useful than guessing asset filenames.
- Devise route prefixes are model-derived: `/users/sign_in` is the default but the model could be `Account`, `Admin`, `Member`, etc. Check HTML links, the import map, or error messages to discover the actual model name before assuming `/users/`.
- Action Cable at `/cable` requires a WebSocket upgrade. `curl` will receive `426 Upgrade Required` — this is expected and confirms the endpoint exists. Do not treat it as a probe failure; note it and use a WS client to interact.
- Hashed asset filenames (Propshaft digests, Sprockets fingerprints) cannot be guessed. Always extract the real filename from an HTML `<script src>` or `<link href>` tag first, then fetch it — never construct asset URLs from patterns alone.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"resources :" language:ruby path:config/routes.rb` | Rails route definitions |
| `"namespace :api" language:ruby` | API namespace declarations |
| `"mount ActionCable" language:ruby` | Action Cable mount points |

### Example Queries for Rails

```bash
# Search for custom API routes/endpoints
site:github.com "Rails" "api" filetype:rb "routes"

# Search for authentication patterns
site:github.com "Rails" "auth" "devise" language:ruby

# Search for configuration files with endpoint definitions
site:github.com "Rails" "config/routes" "endpoint" language:ruby

# Search for custom post types, taxonomies, or extensions
site:github.com "Rails" "register" "custom" language:ruby
```

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

Use these Google search queries to discover exposed endpoints, configuration files, and documentation for this framework.

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:/assets/application-` | Rails compiled JS/CSS assets with digest hashes |
| `site:{domain} inurl:authenticity_token` | Rails CSRF token fields in HTML forms |
| `site:{domain} inurl:/users/sign_in` | Rails Devise authentication login paths |
| `site:{domain} "csrf-token"` | Rails CSRF meta tags in page head |

### Complete Dork List for Rails

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:/api/
site:{domain} inurl:/v1/
site:{domain} inurl:/users/sign_in

# Framework-specific paths
site:{domain} inurl:/assets/application-
site:{domain} inurl:authenticity_token

# Configuration files
site:{domain} filetype:rb "routes.rb"
site:{domain} filetype:yml "database"

# Documentation/leaks
site:{domain} "Rails" "api" "endpoint"
site:{domain} "csrf-token" "meta"

# Admin/debug paths
site:{domain} inurl:/rails/info/properties
site:{domain} inurl:/sidekiq
```

## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib, base64
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
import mmh3 2>/dev/null || pip install mmh3
# Or use: python3 -c "import mmh3; print(mmh3.hash(data))"
print('Favicon hash needed for Shodan search: icon_hash')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
